name: asarum canadense
mechanism: Likely mediated in part by flavonoid/polyphenol antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity
interactions: May potentiate stimulant effects when combined with sympathomimetic agents.
safetyNotes: Potentially toxic; avoid unsupervised use; Avoid with gastrointestinal disorders

sources.title: PubMed search: Asarum canadense
sources.note: Literature index for pharmacology and safety evidence

description: Wild Ginger has been used by Native American tribes as a ceremonial herb and stimulant, as well as a seasoning substitute.
